fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but unprofitable and trading at a high multiple, with significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 44.8%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — very strong top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 441.7% — extremely high expected growth.
PEG ratio 0.78 — suggests growth may be outpacing valuation.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — the company is not profitable.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — poor returns on capital.
Forward P/E 33.20 — high multiple for an unprofitable company.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-445.58M — burning cash significantly.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) saw its stock price tumble nearly 9% on Friday, contributing to a broader cooling off in the optics sector. This follows recent analyses questioning whether the stock's significant AI-driven gains are already priced in, despite strong transceiver orders and substantial analyst growth estimates. The company's recent earnings report showed a slight miss on EPS, adding to the mixed sentiment.
